column10: Ballet shoes are light, soft, smooth shoes that dancers wear on their feet when they do a kind of dancing called ballet. Ballet shoes are usually made from leather, canvas, or satin which can be soft, smooth, and silky. The shoes fit snug so the dancer can feel the floor as they dance and move in careful, graceful ways. There are also special stiff ballet shoes called pointe shoes that let trained dancers balance on their tip-toes. People danced ballet more than 500 years ago in Italy. People have been making and using ballet shoes for about 300 years in France. Dancers all over the world use ballet shoes today.
